title: DSDS Document Block — Scoped Unions
description: Defines scoped document block unions for each artifact type. Every piece of structured docs attached to an artifact — guidelines, anatomy, API specs, variant definitions, state descriptions, accessibility specs, purpose, design specs, principles, scales, motion, content, and interactions — is a document block object with a `type` discriminator. Each artifact type accepts only relevant document block types. This scoping prevents attaching irrelevant docs to an artifact (e.g., an API spec on a style). Express internal artifact relationships through the unified `links` array on each entity rather than through a dedicated document block type.

generalDocumentBlock.description: A document block type available on all artifact types. General document blocks cover cross-cutting concerns that apply regardless of artifact kind.

oneOf.description: Actionable usage rules paired with rationale. Tells the reader what to do (or not do) and why.
Scenario-driven guidance for when to use and when not to use the artifact.
Structured accessibility specs — keyboard interactions, ARIA attributes, screen reader behavior, focus management, color contrast, and motion considerations.
Structured content guidelines — recommended action labels with definitions, usage context, and cross-references, plus localization and internationalization considerations.

componentDocumentBlock.description: A document block type accepted on component artifacts. Includes component-specific types (anatomy, API, variants, states, design specs) and all general types.

oneOf.description: The visual structure of the component — named sub-elements (parts), their associated design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.

The code-level interface — configurable properties, emitted events, content slots, CSS custom properties, CSS shadow parts, data attributes, and public methods. For multi-platform components, include one API document block per platform.
Events emitted by the component — trigger conditions, return shapes, DOM behavior (bubbling, cancelability, composed), and lifecycle metadata.

All dimensions of visual or behavioral variation — each axis (size, emphasis, etc.) with its enumerated values, token overrides, previews, and purpose. Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
All interactive states the component can enter — hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, etc. — with triggers, token overrides, and previews.
Measurable visual specs — token inventory, spacing relationships, dimension constraints, typography settings, and responsive behavior.

styleDocumentBlock.description: A document block type accepted on style artifacts. Includes style-specific types (principles, scales, motion) and all general types.

oneOf.description: High-level guiding principles that shape decision-making for the visual domain — what the system believes and what constraints it accepts.
An ordered sequence of values forming a visual scale — a type scale, spacing scale, elevation scale, or similar progression. Each step references a design token.
Named easing curves with cubic-bezier functions (aligned with DTCG cubicBezier type), recommended durations, and usage guidance for the motion system.

patternDocumentBlock.description: A document block type accepted on pattern artifacts. Includes pattern-specific types (interactions) and shared structural types (anatomy, variants, states). Also includes all general types.

oneOf.description: The interaction flow of the pattern — an ordered sequence of steps describing triggers, system responses, and component behavior.
The visual structure of the pattern — named sub-elements (e.g., image, title, body, primary action), their associated design tokens, and optional annotated diagrams.
Dimensions of variation within the pattern — sub-types, modes, or configurations (e.g., 'no-data', 'user-action', and 'error-management' variants). Optional exclusions document invalid combinations.
States the pattern can enter — populated, empty, loading, error, etc. — with triggers, visual changes, and optional previews.
Events emitted during the pattern's interaction flow — trigger conditions, return shapes, and lifecycle metadata.

tokenDocumentBlock.description: A document block type accepted on token and token group artifacts. Tokens use only general document block types — they do not have component-specific, style-specific, or pattern-specific docs.
